title: Presidential Recall — Emergency Declared to Delay Referendum
description: The Electoral Commission certifies a valid recall petition under Article III Section 14.3. The President responds by declaring a national emergency for the purpose of delaying the certified recall referendum, in direct violation of Article III Section 14.11(a). A court voids the emergency declaration. The Electoral Commission holds the referendum within the required 90-day window. The recall fails to meet the 60% participation threshold under Section 14.7, and the President is retained.
